# omni_cluster (Resource)

Manages an Omni cluster. Machine sets, machines and config patches are managed by their own resources (`omni_machine_set`, `omni_machine_set_node`, `omni_config_patch`).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "omni_cluster" "example" {
  name               = "example"
  kubernetes_version = "1.36.2"
  talos_version      = "1.13.5"

  backup_interval = "1h"

  features = {
    disk_encryption       = true
    enable_workload_proxy = true
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `kubernetes_version` (String) The Kubernetes version to run, in semver format (e.g. `1.36.2`).
- `name` (String) The cluster name. This is the resource ID and is immutable; changing it replaces the cluster.
- `talos_version` (String) The Talos version to run, in semver format (e.g. `1.13.5`).

### Optional

- `backup_interval` (String) Interval between automatic etcd backups, as a Go duration string (e.g. `1h`). When unset or zero, etcd backups are disabled for this cluster.
- `features` (Attributes) Cluster-wide feature flags. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--features))

<a id="nestedatt--features"></a>
### Nested Schema for `features`

Optional:

- `disk_encryption` (Boolean) Enable disk encryption (KMS). Requires Talos >= 1.5.0.
- `enable_workload_proxy` (Boolean) Enable the workload service proxy.
- `use_embedded_discovery_service` (Boolean) Use the discovery service embedded in Omni instead of the public one.
